purpose: Test whether graph/state role signal can be retained in the existing strict-generator trainable path without damaging heldout LM, body-transition, body-action, or body-operand objectives.
code_change: Added optional train-split inverse-sqrt role-frequency class balancing for body-action and body-operand auxiliary targets. The strict_direct_body_emission_path_v1 profile now enables the balance switch, and reports record role factors.
interpretation: Lower auxiliary weights keep the base heldout LM improving and retain a small body-operand improvement.
Body-transition and body-action heads still regress, so neither checkpoint is routeable.
Class-balanced role factors are computed and recorded, but this one-epoch 64-row canary does not move heldout metrics relative to the lower-weight canary.
The next implementation should redesign action/transition supervision around localized semantic-IR/state-machine events or run a larger controlled balance sweep before any strict decode replay.
no_claim_boundary: This is not a learned-generation capability win, public-transfer claim, or production-route claim. No public rows, runtime external inference, fallback/template/router/tool credit, or deterministic repair credit is claimed.
next_required_work: Do not route either checkpoint. Keep role class balancing as an auditable option, but make body-transition and body-action supervision semantically sharper before strict private replay.
